key_points: The number system includes natural numbers, whole numbers, integers and rational numbers
Integers extend the number system to include negative numbers
Rational numbers can represent fractions, terminating decimals and repeating decimals
Numbers can be compared and represented using a number line
Operations such as addition and subtraction can be performed on integers and rational numbers
formulas: Rational number form → p/q where p and q are integers and q ≠ 0
Absolute value → |a| gives the distance from zero
exam_tips: Use the number line to compare positive and negative integers
Remember that every integer can be written as a rational number
Check the denominator when writing rational numbers
Understand the difference between terminating and repeating decimals
